name: itsolpowers
description: ITSOL engineering skills and Claude Code subagents for task intake, repo memory, current technology documentation research, ML/AI engineering, application technology migration, React 19/Next.js frontend, TanStack Query React/Next cache workflows, agent-browser frontend dogfood, Expo React Native mobile apps, Electron and Tauri desktop apps, SQL Server/.NET data access, file-based functional planning, subagent execution, TDD, UI/UX frontend, implementation, debugging, review, security, infrastructure, and production readiness.

interface.shortDescription: ITSOL workflows, current docs, UI/UX, migration, planning, TDD, and review.
interface.longDescription: ITSOL Powers provides Codex skills for routing engineering tasks across repo memory and monorepo policy, current technology documentation and version research, ML/AI engineering workflows for project planning, data evaluation, training experiments, LLM/RAG, model serving, MLOps, drift, retraining, model review and Rust ML/LLM precedence, UI/UX frontend workflows, React 19 and Next.js App Router work, Server/Client Components, TanStack Query v5 React/Next query keys, SSR hydration, mutations, invalidation, optimistic updates, auth cache and tenant isolation, agent-browser local dogfood, browser-based reproduction, pre-QA validation, interaction debugging, console/network/HAR/trace/Web Vitals evidence, redacted screenshots and videos, QA handoff reports, production-safe browser smoke checks, Expo React Native mobile app architecture, Expo SDK versioning, Expo Router, development builds, CNG/prebuild, app config, permissions, storage/offline, native modules, EAS Build, EAS Update/OTA, credentials, store release, mobile debugging and mobile review, Electron desktop app architecture, main/preload/renderer boundaries, IPC contracts, BrowserWindow hardening, auto-update, packaging, code signing, Tauri Rust desktop app architecture, commands, capabilities, permissions, WebView behavior, sidecars, updater signatures, release distribution, API/cache/forms, generated OpenAPI clients, design systems, accessibility, responsive behavior, frontend testing and QA, application technology migration and rewrites, SQL Server/.NET data access with EF Core, Dapper, stored procedures, migrations, Query Store and operations debugging, file-based functional planning, approved business and technical plan markdown artifacts, approved technical fix plans for bugs, subagent-driven execution, TDD, implementation, debugging, self-review, security review, infrastructure design, deployment readiness, and incident analysis using ITSOL engineering workflows.
defaultPrompt: Use ITSOL Powers to classify this task and choose the right skills.
Initialize or read .itsol.md repo memory before choosing project-specific TDD, verification, or monorepo workflow policy.
When planning or reviewing framework, SDK, runtime, package, generated client, or API-dependent work, verify current official documentation and repo-pinned versions first.

For machine learning and AI engineering work, route through ML project planning, data/evaluation, training/experiments, LLM/RAG, or serving/MLOps/review skills; prefer rust-ml-llm skills first for Rust/Rig/Candle-specific code.
For React 19 and Next.js work, route through React/Next implementation, review, debugging, App Router/rendering, API/cache/forms, or quality/security skills as appropriate.

For TanStack Query v5 in React/Next.js, use focused implementation, review, or debugging skills for query keys, SSR hydration, mutations, invalidation, optimistic updates, auth cache, tenant isolation, and tests.

For agent-browser local dogfood, browser-based reproduction, evidence capture, or pre-QA validation, route through agent-browser workflow, interaction debugging, diagnostics/evidence, QA reporting, or security/production safety skills; check the installed CLI version/help and load local core/dogfood guidance when the CLI supports it.

For Expo React Native mobile app work, route through Expo implementation, review, debugging, security/permissions, or EAS release/OTA skills for Expo SDK, Expo Router, development builds, CNG/prebuild, app config, permissions, storage/offline, native modules, EAS Build, EAS Submit, EAS Update, runtime versions/channels, OTA rollout/rollback, CI, store release, and mobile diagnostics.

For Electron desktop app work, route through Electron implementation, review, debugging, security hardening, or release/distribution skills for main/preload/renderer boundaries, IPC, BrowserWindow, storage, auto-update, packaging, signing, and packaged-app diagnostics.

For Tauri desktop app work, route through Tauri implementation, review, debugging, security/capabilities, or release/distribution skills for Rust/frontend boundaries, commands, invoke/events, capabilities, permissions, WebView differences, sidecars, updater, bundling, signing, and platform diagnostics.
For visible frontend work, use UI/UX skills for design-system consistency, component architecture, view states, responsiveness, accessibility, performance, tests, and UI review.
For application rewrites or technology migrations, inventory behavior, choose a safe migration strategy, and plan incremental slices before implementation.
For functional work, write and require approved Business Plan and Technical Plan markdown files before implementation.
For subagent-driven work, split tasks, set concurrency, review each implementation with a separate subagent, and commit reviewed slices.
For bugfixes, gather evidence and require an approved Technical Fix Plan before implementation.
Use ITSOL TDD workflow before changing production code.
Review this change against ITSOL security and infrastructure practices.
Debug this issue using ITSOL task and incident workflows.
